title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: Sports Day
questions.text: Because heavy rain is expected, sports day will begin in the gym instead of on the field. Races may move outside later if the weather improves. Parents should still arrive at the usual time.
questions.question: What has changed about sports day?

options.A: It will start in a different place.
options.B: Parents should arrive later than planned.
options.C: All races have been cancelled for today.

questions.explanation: The event will start in the gym instead of on the field.

questions.heading: Message from Leo to Hannah
questions.text: Hannah, I found a better train home after the match. It leaves twenty minutes later, but it goes straight to our station. I think it’s worth waiting because we won’t need to change trains.
questions.question: What is Leo suggesting?

options.A: They take a later but easier journey.
options.B: They leave the match twenty minutes early.
options.C: They change trains at another station.

questions.explanation: Leo suggests waiting for a later direct train.

questions.heading: Café Sandwiches
questions.text: Today’s chicken sandwiches contain mayonnaise and mustard. Ask staff for the vegetarian option if you prefer not to eat meat. Sandwiches are made this morning and should be eaten today.
questions.question: What information does the label give?

options.A: There is another choice for some customers.
options.B: All sandwiches are suitable for vegetarians.
options.C: Customers should keep sandwiches until tomorrow.

questions.explanation: Customers who do not eat meat can ask for a vegetarian option.

questions.heading: From: Adam / Subject: Group project
questions.text: I’ve written the introduction for our project, but it’s probably too long. Could you read it tonight and mark any sentences we don’t really need? Then I’ll make it shorter before the lesson.
questions.question: What does Adam want the reader to do?

options.A: Help him reduce part of the project.
options.B: Write the introduction before the lesson.
options.C: Add more information to the project.

questions.explanation: Adam asks the reader to mark unnecessary sentences so he can shorten the introduction.

questions.heading: Town Swimming Lessons
questions.text: Swimming lessons for teenagers are available on Monday and Wednesday evenings. The Monday class is for beginners, while Wednesday is for swimmers who can already swim twenty-five metres without stopping.
questions.question: Who should choose the Wednesday class?

options.A: Teenagers with some swimming ability already.
options.B: Teenagers who have never swum before.
options.C: Teenagers who want lessons every evening.

questions.explanation: Wednesday is for swimmers who can already swim twenty-five metres.
